title: Setting Widget Properties
text: Setting Widget Properties You can set widget properties in several ways—in the initialization file used by the application, in the IDE using the Properties Inspector, or in script. And you can set them in modeled entities, in component templates, in components, and in script. This provides an inheritance mechanism which enables you to define a common appearance for widgets, but which is flexible enough to be overridden when required. Uniface relies on object inheritance and a defined order of precedence to ensure that it can apply the appropriate property value at runtime. For more information, see Property Values at Runtime . Related Links Widgets In Uniface, widgets are user interface controls. They can display data values, control how values are modified, and determine the appearance of those objects. Most widgets are field-level widgets. $properties Return or set the current widget properties of a field. $fieldproperties Return or set the current widget properties of an instance of a field. $entityproperties Return or set the current representation properties of an entity. $CurEntProperties Set properties for an inner entity within the current occurrence of its parent entity. $occproperties Returns or sets the properties of an occurrence. getProperty() Returns the value of a property of this Uniface data object. getProperties() Returns the properties of this Uniface data object. setProperty() Sets the specified widget property to a given value. setProperties() Sets widget properties for a Uniface data object in JavaScript. clearProperty() Restores the initial value of the specified property. clearProperties() Restores the initial widget properties of the Uniface data object.
